document: Introduction To Internet Of Things JAN 2026

questions.questionText: What is the full form of loT? [cite: 5]
questions.explanation: The full form of IoT is Internet of Things. It refers to a vast network of physical objects-"things"-embedded with sensors, software, and other technologies to connect and exchange data with other devices and systems over the internet. [cite: 10]

questions.questionText: The "Source Address" filed in the IPv6 header is of [cite: 11]
questions.explanation: In an IPv6 header, the source and destination addresses are each 128 bits long, which is four times the size of an IPv4 address. [cite: 30] This massive increase in length provides a significantly larger pool of unique IP addresses (2128) to accommodate the growing number of devices connected to the global internet. [cite: 31]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are not an enabler of loT? [cite: 32]
questions.explanation: All the listed options-RFID, Sensors and Actuators, and Cloud Computing- are critical enablers of the Internet of Things. [cite: 47] RFID and sensors collect data from the physical world, actuators allow devices to perform actions, and cloud computing provides the necessary infrastructure to process and store the massive amounts of data generated by these devices. [cite: 48]

questions.questionText: The gateway is tasked with assigning a unique address to each of the field devices or sensors within its jurisdiction. [cite: 49]
questions.explanation: In many loT architectures, the Gateway acts as a local coordinator that manages field devices and sensors. It is responsible for translating protocols and assigning unique local addresses or identifiers to ensure data from each specific sensor is correctly tracked and routed within its network jurisdiction. [cite: 62, 63]

questions.questionText: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: The increasing number of devices in IoT is expected to result in an address crunch. [cite: 65, 66]
questions.explanation: As billions of new devices connect to the Internet of Things, the older IPv4 addressing system (which only supports about 4.3 billion addresses) has run out of space, creating an address crunch. [cite: 70] This is the primary reason for the transition to IPv6, which provides a virtually unlimited number of addresses to accommodate future growth. [cite: 71]

questions.questionText: In ....... a node/network is connected to multiple networks for improved reliability. [cite: 72, 73, 74]
questions.explanation: The specific concept described-connecting a node or network to multiple service providers or networks for redundancy and reliability is known as Multi-homing. [cite: 85] While "Multi-clouding" involves using multiple cloud services, it refers to distributed computing resources rather than the foundational network connectivity structure used for improved link reliability. [cite: 86]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT a function of an IoT Gateway? [cite: 87]
options: a. Connects the IoT LAN to a WAN [cite: 88]
b. Can implement several LAN and WAN [cite: 89]
questions.explanation: In IoT architecture, both connecting the LAN to a WAN and implementing various LAN/WAN protocols (like Wi-Fi, Zigbee, or Cellular) are core functions of an IoT Gateway. [cite: 92] Since the question asks which of these is NOT a function, and both are indeed primary functions, "Neither" is the correct choice. [cite: 93]

questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are incorrect? Statement I: An actuator is a component of a machine or system that moves or controls the mechanism or the system. Statement II: An actuator requires a control signal and a source of energy. [cite: 94, 95, 96]
questions.explanation: Statement I accurately defines an actuator as the part of a system responsible for physical movement or control, essentially acting as the "muscles" of a machine. [cite: 106] Statement II is also correct because actuators cannot function alone; [cite: 107] they must receive a specific control signal (instruction) and draw from an energy source (like electricity or hydraulics) to perform their task. [cite: 108]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT a function of an IoT LAN? [cite: 109]
questions.explanation: An IoT LAN (Local Area Network) is designed for short-range communication within a limited area, such as a home or building. [cite: 113] Long-range, global communication and worldwide connections are characteristic functions of a WAN (Wide Area Network) or the Cloud, rather than a local network. [cite: 114]

questions.questionText: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: The word "Transducer" is a collective term used for both Sensors and Actuators. [cite: 115, 116]
questions.explanation: A transducer is any device that converts energy from one form to another. [cite: 123] Since a sensor converts physical parameters (like heat) into electrical signals, and an actuator converts electrical signals back into physical action (like movement), they are both technically types of transducers working in opposite directions. [cite: 124]

questions.questionText: Analog Sensors produce a discontinuous output signal or voltage, which is generally proportional to the quantity being measured. [cite: 125]
questions.explanation: Analog sensors produce a continuous output signal or voltage, not a discontinuous one. [cite: 132] The signal varies smoothly and is directly proportional to the physical quantity (like temperature or pressure) being measured, whereas a discontinuous or discrete signal is characteristic of digital sensors. [cite: 133]

questions.questionText: If the output signal differs from the correct value by a constant, the sensor has an...... [cite: 136]
questions.explanation: In sensor technology, an offset error occurs when the output signal is shifted from the actual value by a constant amount across the entire measurement range. [cite: 148] This constant displacement is also commonly referred to as bias, and it can usually be corrected through a process called calibration by subtracting the known constant error. [cite: 149]

questions.questionText: State True or False. Pneumatic actuators enable the production of large forces from a relatively larger pressure changes. [cite: 150, 151]
questions.explanation: Pneumatic actuators are actually designed to produce large forces from small pressure changes. [cite: 160] Because they use compressed air, which is highly compressible, they are very responsive to slight adjustments in pressure, allowing them to control mechanical movements efficiently without needing massive pressure spikes. [cite: 161]

questions.questionText: A sensor node is made up of which of the following? [cite: 163]
questions.explanation: A typical sensor node (also called a mote) is an integrated system-on-a-chip that requires several components to function: sensors to gather environmental data, a processing unit (microcontroller) to manage and store that data, and a power unit (usually batteries) to keep the device running. [cite: 169] Additionally, it usually includes a radio transceiver for wireless communication with other nodes or a gateway. [cite: 170]

questions.questionText: What is the full form of SMP in the context of IoT actuators? [cite: 171]
questions.explanation: In the context of actuators, Shape Memory Polymers are smart materials that can be deformed and then return to their original shape when triggered by an external stimulus, such as heat or light. [cite: 182] They are highly valued in IoT for creating "soft actuators" that are lightweight, flexible, and capable of complex movements in robotics or medical devices. [cite: 183]
